props.text: TL;DR: Blockchain.com ships 30-second BTC binary contracts as a new 'skill-based' product, a federal judge permanently blocks Arizona from prosecuting Kalshi on preemption grounds, and Kalshi's April taker volume overtakes Polymarket for the first time at $5.42B vs $1.99B.

props.title: Blockchain.com launches SnapMarkets — 30-second BTC binary contracts from $1

props.text: Users wager whether bitcoin's price rises or falls in 30-second windows; live chat, streak tracking, global leaderboard; non-custodial wallet by default. Not available in the US or UK. Marketed as 'skill-based' — structurally a binary option, the class banned for retail in the EU/UK.
Why it matters: Second venue selling sub-minute BTC contracts after Kalshi KXBTC. More fast-resolution venues mean more cross-venue convergence data and more candidate legs for short-window arb.

props.title: Federal judge permanently blocks Arizona's criminal case against Kalshi

props.text: Judge Liburdi made permanent the April TRO, ruling federal law preempts state gambling statutes for CFTC-registered exchanges. Arizona AG Kris Mayes is barred from prosecuting the 20 misdemeanor counts; four of those targeted Kalshi's 2026/2028 election contracts. CFTC Chair Mike Selig: 'the CFTC has full jurisdiction over prediction markets.'
Why it matters: First district-level permanent ruling on prediction-market preemption — sets the precedent every other state suit (Connecticut, Illinois, Nevada, NJ) will be measured against.

props.title: Kalshi overtakes Polymarket on April monthly volume — $5.42B vs $1.99B taker

props.text: Sector taker volume hit $8.6B; in notional terms, Kalshi posted $14.8B vs Polymarket's $9B (plus $1.26B from Polymarket US). Polymarket still led on unique users — 678,342 vs Kalshi's roughly 80,000 — putting Kalshi's per-user volume near 8x Polymarket's. Sports drove most of the Kalshi lead.

Why it matters: First month Kalshi has outrun Polymarket on monthly volume, compounding with the Arizona ruling. Liquidity weight is shifting to the regulated venue; convergence-trade fills get easier on the Kalshi side, thinner on Polymarket outside political and India-election books.
